content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
• 
1102 Q Street• Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811 
r (916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Krishan Chopra 
Senior Assistant City Attorney 
500 Castro Street 
Post Office Box 7540 
Mountain View, CA 94041 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-17-291 
Dear Mr. Chopra: 
January 12, 2017 
This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of Mountain View City 
Councilmember Margaret Abe-Koga regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political 
Reform Act (the "Act").1 Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest 
provisions of the Act and not under other general conflict ofinterest prohibitions such as common 
law conflict of interest or Section 1090. Please also note that we are not a finder of fact when 
rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) I FPPC Ops. 71 ), and any advice we provide assumes your 
facts are complete and accurate. 
QUESTION 
Does the Act prohibit Councilmember Abe-Koga from taking part in governmental 
decisions relating to a transfer of unused development rights by the Los Altos School District for 
project sites in various locations throughout the City, including within the East Whisman Precise 
Plan area where the corporate headquarters of the Councilmember's former employer is located? 
CONCLUSION 
No. The Act does not prohibit the Councilmember from taking part in those decisions 
because they would not have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on any of the 
Councilmember's financial interests based on the facts presented. 
1 The Political Refonn Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated, The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


FACTS 
File No. A-17-291 
Page No. 2 
The Los Altos School District serves the elementary and intennediate educational needs of 
the residents of the City of Mountain View and other nearby communities. The District 
administrates a school within the area of the City's San Antonio Precise Plan (SAPP), and the SAPP 
authorizes transfers of unused development rights. The District proposes to sell its unused 
development rights from its school site (the "sending site") within the SAPP area to allow the 
purchaser to transfer and utilize those development rights at another property (the "receiving site") 
located in another area of the City. The proceeds from the sale of these transferred development 
rights would fund the District's acquisition of land for a new school site. 
Developers with potential projects in other areas of the City, including projects within the 
East Whisman Precise Plan (EWPP) area, have expressed interest in purchasing the District's 
unused development rights. After the transfer, in what is known as a "gatekeeper" process, the 
developers of the receiving sites seek the City Council's authorization to have City staff process the 
land use entitlement applications associated with the receiving-site projects. If City staff is 
authorized to process those applications, a developer would be able to submit a planning application 
to the City for a proposed project at a receiving site and pursue the approval of that project through 
the normal planning process. 
Synopsys is a large international technology corporation and a leader in the electronic 
design automation industry. The Councilmember was employed by Synopsys until July 7, 2017, 
when she received final compensation from Synopsys of $500 or more. Synopsys is listed on the 
Nasdaq Stock Market and has a market capitalization of more than $13 billion. 
Synopsys' corporate headquarters is located within the EWPP area. Of the eight receiving 
sites associated with the District's transfer, five are within the EWPP area, and three are located 
elsewhere in the City. Four of the receiving sites within the EWPP area are zoned for office land 
uses, and the fifth is zoned for high density residential. The closest receiving site within the EWPP 
area is located approximately 1,400 feet away from Synopsys' corporate headquarters. Synopsys is 
not seeking to purchase any of the District's unused development rights, or to develop any of the 
receiving sites. 
On January 16, 2018, the City Council will undertake the gatekeeper process, and will 
detennine whether to authorize City staff to process developers' land-use entitlement applications 
for projects located at the receiving sites and enabled by the District transfer. 
ANALYSIS 
The Act's conflict of interest provisions prohibit a public official from taking part in a 
governmental decision that would have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on one or 
more of the official's financial interests. (Sections 87100 and 87103.) Section 87103 sets forth an 
official's financial interests that may give rise to a disqualifying conflict of interest under the Act. 
An official has a financial interest in a source of income aggregating $500 or more in value 
promised to or received by the official within 12 months prior to the time when the decision at issue 
is made. (Section 87103(c).) The Councilmember has a source of income interest in Synopsys, her 


File No. A-17-291 
Page No. 3 
fonner employer, through July 7, 2017, when she received final compensation from Synopsys of 
$500 or more.2 
Foreseeability and Materiality 
Regulation 18 70 I sets forth the Act's standards for detennining whether a decision's 
financial effect on an official's financial interest is reasonably foreseeable. Subdivision (a) of that 
regulation provides that a decision's financial effect on an official's financial interest is presumed to 
be reasonably foreseeable if the interest is a named party in, or the subject of, the decision. 
Subdivision (a) also provides that an official's interest is explicitly involved in a decision if the 
interest is a named party in or the subject of the decision, and that an interest is the subject of a 
proceeding if it involves the issuance, renewal, approval, denial, or revocation of any license, 
pennit, or other entitlement to, or contract with, the interest. 
Synopsys is not seeking to purchase any of the District's unused development rights, or to 
develop any of the receiving-site projects. Because neither Synopsys nor the Councilmember's 
personal finances are explicitly involved in the decisions relating to the District's transfer, 
Regulation 1870l(a) does not apply. 
Regulation 1870l(b) provides the foreseeability standard applicable to an official's interest 
which is not explicitly involved in the decisions at issue: 
In general, if the financial effect can be recognized as a realistic possibility and 
more than hypothetical or theoretical, it is reasonably foreseeable. If the 
financial result cannot be expected absent extraordinary circumstances not 
subject to the public official's control, it is not reasonably foreseeable. 
Regulation 18702.3 sets forth the materiality standards applicable to a decision's effect on 
an official's interest in a source of income. Paragraph (4) of subdivision (a) of that regulation 
provides that the effect on the source of income interest is material if the source is a business entity 
that will be financially affected under the standards applied to a business interest in Regulation 
18702.1. For a business interest that is not explicitly involved in the decisions at issue, Regulation 
18702.1 (b) provides that those decisions' financial effect on that business interest is material if it 
"would contribute to a change in the price of the business entity's publicly traded stock." 
Synopsys is a large international technology corporation, is listed on the Nasdaq Stock 
Market, and has a market capitalization of more than $13 billion. Unlike a brick and mortar retail or 
restaurant business which draws its customers from the surrounding neighborhood, Synopsys is an 
international business that does not rely on business from the surrounding neighborhood. Therefore, 
even if the decisions relating to the District's transfer would eventually result in the development of 
real properties within the EWPP area in close proximity to Synopsys' corporate headquarters, there 
is no indication from the facts presented that the development of those properties "would contribute 
to a change in the price of [Synopsys'] publicly traded stock." 
2 An official always has a financial interest in his or her personal finances and those of immediate family 
members. (See Section 87103.) However, nothing in the facts provided indicate a potential effect on the 
Councilmember's personal finances. 


File No. A-17-291 
Page No. 4 
Accordingly, we conclude that the Act does not prohibit the Councilmember from taking 
part in decisions relating to the transfer because they would not have a reasonably foreseeable 
material financial effect on any of the Councilmember's financial interests based on the facts 
presented. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
MFC:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Brian G. Lau 
Assistant General Counsel 
By: 
Matthew F. Christy 
Counsel, Legal Division
